mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 1% signal aggressive monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset valuations. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as lower rates compress risk premiums.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% on 2008-12-01 indicates extreme easing
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 1%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during easing regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 7% signals labor market deterioration and recessionary pressure, triggering equity risk-off behavior. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.3% on 2008-12-01 indicates severe labor market stress
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 60% of triggered instances when UNRATE > 7%, or the mechanism produces positive returns in more than 40% of recession-regime observations.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month signals weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate earnings expectations. This interaction-driven mechanism combines RSAFS weakness with elevated unemployment to amplify negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 343242M on 2008-12-01 reflects demand collapse
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 65% of joint-triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below 95 index points signals supply-side contraction and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days during recession regimes when supply shocks amplify earnings uncertainty.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.1173 on 2008-11-01 vintage, revised to 93.4715 indicating severe contraction
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 45% of triggered instances during recession regimes, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window in supply-shock periods.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 650 thousand units signals collapsing residential investment and negative wealth effects on consumer spending. This interaction-driven mechanism combines HOUST weakness with declining retail sales to predict negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 650 thousand units AND RSAFS declines > 1% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 625.0 on 2008-11-01 indicates housing market collapse
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 70% of joint-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not significant (p > 0.10) in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 215 index points signals deflationary pressure and monetary policy accommodation. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as low inflation reduces real discount rates and supports equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 213.06 on 2008-11-01 indicates deflationary environment
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of observations when CPIAUCSL < 215, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) declines month-over-month by more than 500 thousand persons signals severe labor market shock and recessionary dynamics. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice earnings and growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135489.0 on 2008-12-01 vintage, revised to 134847.0 indicating large job losses
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 65% of triggered instances, or the mechanism produces positive returns in more than 35% of severe employment loss observations.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both decline simultaneously signal synchronized supply and labor contraction, amplifying recessionary signals. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days when both supply and labor markets deteriorate together.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1% month-over-month AND PAYEMS declines > 300 thousand persons month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Synchronized contraction in both series on 2008-12-01 indicates broad economic deterioration
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 70% of joint-triggered instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 2-year rolling window.
